project_title: Analyst Workstation - eight-page interactive AI-augmented intelligence-analysis handbook
runtime_target: interactive analysis application with SAT registry, baseline-vs-reviewed output, confidence delta, and release decision
operational_end_state: An analyst can run an AI output through structured analytic techniques, record human corrections, compare confidence, identify residual disagreement, and decide whether to release, narrow, or block the result.
core_modules: SAT method registry | baseline AI output intake | structured review board | human correction ledger | confidence delta tracker | release decision gate
source_strategy: Use sources to test human-in-the-loop analytic value: structured technique selection, evidence weighting, confidence change, AI-output correction, residual disagreement, and release recommendation.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | human-AI delta test: structured human review changes, narrows, blocks, or justifies output
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Build one SAT comparison board: baseline AI answer, structured technique pass, human delta, confidence boundary, and release decision.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
